author: Midwest-AI-Solutions
title: dimension_coverage tautological metric — len(x)/max(len(x),1) always 1.0 or 0.0
notes: Confirmed: core.py:373 computes len(assessments)/max(len(assessments),1) which is mathematically always 1.0 (non-empty) or 0.0 (empty). The metric is intended to measure partial dimension coverage but is structurally incapable of it. Propagates through 4 downstream consumers (accumulate, merge, display, import). Test at review_commands_cases.py:1035 asserts 1.0 — passes trivially. S207 by @Boehner is a later duplicate.
